title: 3D Botox BROW LIFT INJECTION TUTORIAL
excerpt: I always like to start with the less routine injections first so you know your treal bell all day this is a little bit rarer so I'm going to do that first while I'm thinking about it as a special area we're going to do three injections now remember what I said about depth here th…

segments.text: I always like to start with the less routine injections first so you know your treal bell all day this is a little bit rarer so I'm going to do that first while I'm thinking about it as a special area we're going to do three injections now remember what I said about depth here this needs to be superficial might be a little bit safer if you're angled slightly away from the eye until you've got your Anatomy really dialed in so superficial injection just underneath and I only need to go just basically underneath the dermis and the dermis is super thin here so it's probably about point2 of a millimeter I do not this does not need to be a deep injection so I'm going to just pop in like that that's it elevate the skin you can see a little bit of blanching that's a good sign I'm in the right place and that's just one unit same again there we go three injections just underneath here um I sometimes just want

to make myself feel safer by Rolling away from the eye I don't think it does anything but it feels [Music] better there's a debate always about whether you should be pointing away from the eye towards the eye when you first start you think you should definitely be pointing away from the eye because it just gives you a bit less leeway a bit more leeway if you make a mistake the biggest issue is that people go too deep they create a track even if they come right out to the right place they end up with a path for Bim toxin towards where you might get a side effect so it makes sense to inject in a direction where even if you go too deep it doesn't matter so here if I go too deep I'm just treating Temple there's nothing really there to cause any issues so a little injection just here two units you can also stand on the other side of the bed and lean over your patient so next injection point we're going to do the so the lella is a risk

for osis and one simple step I recommend you're doing is just putting orbital pressure so you basically squeeze the tissue where the tract would have to be in order to get BME toxin into the orbit so a little bit of pressure here the next thing is just to remind yourself of the anatomy the bone is the origin of the corrugated supercilii muscle and it's going towards this insertion point and that's actually how the needle should follow so this is the easiest way to be accurate is to aim for the largest shape if I'm aiming from inferior or Superior I have a smaller area of my that I can be safe in this is much safer because if I even if I'm slightly a degree or two off the muscle is basically going to capture it so insertion point here it's a relatively deep injection start with four units pressure on the globe and then you do the lateral injection three units intermediate depth and not as deep and then

and then superficial right at the end one unit a little lucky roll or bit of pressure away from the eye would you like to try that on the other [Music] side so forehead injections are actually the easiest technically because we spent so much time creating a safety margin here that the basically this the skill bit is done and the injection works pretty well no matter what you do here now there are some Nuance differences which is if you inject intradermally and you get little white BLB that forms then you're not going to get as much of a result cuz you now got botam toxin sitting in the dermis which is then underneath the dermis you have the hypodermis which is a layer of fat then you have the Muscle fascia then you have the muscle itself that you're trying to treat So you you're separating it away from the effective area uh a paper recently came out from

Sebastian kotan which talked about more effect going deep rather than superficial but I've never actually seen anyone inject that superficially that they're getting white blbs cuz it hurts for one thing you get more pain because there's more pressure in the dermis and you see a white bled as you squeeze the blood out of the skin so what I'm actually normally trying to do is hit the muscle itself which means I go through the dermis which basically feels like resistance and then it suddenly eases and then I go a tiny bit more and imagining that to be basically in the muscle basically just through the fascia which is where most of the nerve endings are and that's where I inject if you go deeper and touch the bone that's fine but the problem is you blun in your needle every time you touch the periosteum doesn't hurt as much as you think but um I prefer just trying to hit the muscle that's just my thinking on it um so let's I'll demonstrate what I do it's about 45° angle of Entry once again I'm not actually trying to inject here I'm just injecting underneath it so I'd be going in here and doing two units just through the dermis and

dermis and stop pretty straightforward injection two now you should always clean these little blood spots off as you go because they're much harder to remove once they dried on and in a busy Clinic it actually saves you about 5 minutes which is a lot very simple we're now just repeating the same thing again 45° angle of entry and as you inject feel resistance and then it gets slightly easier two injection points you know you're in the right layer cuz it Doesn't blanch blanching is not good so this is a good example I've hit a little vein here and I'm every time I release to check if it stopped bleeding it just keeps bleeding so I've now made the mistake I'm not going to check again I'm going to hold it for a full minute cuz although this is never going to be anything but a small bruise I try my best to keep bruising to a minimum because I'm just all the time trying to aim for this being a seamless easy process for

my patients not having to put loads of makeup on to cover a little bruise not having a little sore spot so it's worth spending at least a minute just holding [Music] this so finally we'll do two quick little one unit doses [Music] here the forehead done [Music]
